purpose: Verification canon for evaluating the Keon MCP Gateway repository against its claimed product and governance behavior. This is a repo-verification canon, not marketing copy.

owned_surfaces: MCP tool descriptor and call handling
canonical governed envelope preservation in MCP structuredContent
ingress spine append behavior and persistence modes
local development examples and smoke scripts
gateway-specific contracts, schemas, tests, CI, deployment config, and operational docs
non_targets: Do not verify Keon Systems Runtime internals except that the gateway calls the expected runtime contracts and handles runtime responses safely.
Do not verify Cortex internals except that gateway-produced receipts/spine references are shaped and exposed for downstream memory/audit use when claimed.
Do not verify Keon Collective cognition, planning, deliberation, or worker orchestration.
Do not verify website, dashboard, marketing site, pitch deck, or product copy except for false claims inside the gateway repo itself.
Do not require live SaaS connectivity unless the repo explicitly claims live integration and provides live-test configuration.

integration_boundaries.relationship: Gateway delegates policy decision and execution to Runtime. Gateway must enforce authentication, identity binding, scope checks, request shaping, Decide-before-Execute ordering, receipt preservation, fail-closed runtime handling, and governed envelope exposure.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Verify gateway-side call order, request/response contracts, error handling, and evidence preservation. Do not require proving Runtime policy internals in this repo.

integration_boundaries.relationship: Gateway may emit or expose durable ingress spine receipts and evidence references for memory/audit.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Verify gateway append behavior, persistence-mode semantics, spine refs, and receipt linkage where implemented or claimed. Do not verify Cortex storage internals.

integration_boundaries.relationship: Gateway presents standard MCP methods/transports so existing MCP clients can connect with minimal adoption friction.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Verify protocol-level methods, result shape, auth/header/env handling, and client-compatible stdio/HTTP behavior.

capabilities.one_line_description: Universal enforcement layer that intercepts and governs all MCP tool execution, injecting identity, policy, receipts, and causal lineage into every interaction
All MCP tool execution must pass through the governed gateway; there are no direct execution paths or side channels
Existing MCP clients connect without modification and immediately receive governed, receipt-rich execution with no integration overhead
Every tool result is wrapped in a canonical governance envelope containing decision, policy state, receipts, correlation, and verifiable proof artifacts
Automatically emits append-only causal receipts (directive → intent → decision → execution → outcome)
Automatic tenant_id + actor_id binding from JWT or API keys with scope enforcement
Configurable durability (Off / BestEffort / Required) with fail-closed behavior on Required mode

verification_principles: Claims require receipts: docs and README claims are not implementation proof.
Gateway governance is proven by enforced call paths, negative tests, schemas/contracts, and observable result envelopes.
Missing identity, missing scope, runtime failure, invalid request shape, and required spine failures must fail closed.
MCP client compatibility cannot bypass governance.
The gateway may adapt protocol transport, but it must not weaken authorization or receipt preservation.
Any claimed support surface must have tests or executable smoke evidence.
Deferred features must be labeled deferred and must not be claimed as production-ready.

critical: Any direct execution path that bypasses gateway governance or Runtime Decide-before-Execute
Execution proceeds after denial, missing identity, missing scope, invalid receipt, or runtime failure
Required ingress spine mode fails open when configured as Required
Authentication can be bypassed on protected tool call surfaces
Tenant or actor can be silently widened or spoofed
README/public repo claims production support for unimplemented governance behavior
high: Core MCP method or transport claimed but missing or untested
Canonical structuredContent envelope missing decision, receipts, correlation, or status fields
Legacy and standard MCP surfaces diverge in governance enforcement
Origin enforcement absent when browser HTTP access is supported
Schema validation missing for claimed canonical envelopes
medium: Operational docs incomplete for auth modes, env vars, or failure modes
CI does not cover advertised primary workflows
low: Minor docs drift, naming inconsistencies, or incomplete examples that do not affect safety

requirements.title: Official governed MCP gateway surface
requirements.requirement: The repo MUST implement a gateway that fronts governed tool execution and injects identity, policy decision state, receipts, and causal lineage into MCP interactions.
negative_checks: No MCP tool call path may return successful execution without governance metadata.

requirements.title: Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint
requirements.requirement: The gateway MUST support the advertised official MCP Streamable HTTP request/response endpoint, normally POST /mcp.
negative_checks: Unsupported methods must return protocol errors rather than ungoverned fallbacks.

requirements.title: stdio MCP mode
requirements.requirement: If stdio mode is claimed, the gateway MUST support launching as an MCP stdio server without non-protocol stdout noise.
evidence_required: stdio handler tests or smoke script
negative_checks: Startup banners, logs, or diagnostics must not corrupt stdout protocol stream.

requirements.title: Supported MCP methods
requirements.requirement: The gateway MUST implement every advertised MCP method, at minimum initialize, notifications/initialized, ping, tools/list, and tools/call when claimed.
negative_checks: Advertised methods must not be docs-only.

requirements.title: Supported MCP protocol versions
requirements.requirement: The gateway MUST explicitly negotiate or validate the advertised MCP protocol versions and reject or safely handle unsupported versions.
evidence_required: tests for supported and unsupported versions
negative_checks: Unsupported protocol version must not silently downgrade into unsafe execution.

requirements.title: No-bypass governance enforcement
requirements.requirement: All tool execution through the gateway MUST traverse the same governance enforcement path. Standard MCP and legacy surfaces must not provide side-channel execution.
evidence_required: shared service/path for tools/call and legacy invoke
tests comparing standard and legacy governance
static search for direct execution bypasses
negative_checks: No handler may call execution/runtime directly without auth, scope, Decide-before-Execute, and receipt handling.

requirements.title: Decide before Execute orchestration
requirements.requirement: For any effect-bearing tool invocation, the gateway MUST call Runtime Decide before Runtime Execute and MUST NOT Execute when Decide denies, fails, times out, or returns invalid/missing approval.
negative_checks: Execute must not be called after denial, invalid decision, missing receipt, timeout, or runtime failure.

requirements.title: Canonical governed envelope in structuredContent
requirements.requirement: MCP tools/call responses MUST preserve the canonical Keon governed envelope in result.structuredContent, including correlation_id, tool, ok/status, decision, result or denial/error, receipts when available, and isError semantics.
negative_checks: Human-readable content alone is insufficient; structuredContent must carry machine-verifiable governance data.

requirements.title: MCP error semantics
requirements.requirement: Gateway failures, denials, invalid auth, invalid request shapes, and runtime failures MUST return safe MCP error semantics with isError=true or JSON-RPC error as appropriate while preserving available governance context.
negative_checks: Denied or failed governance decisions must not be represented as successful executions.

requirements.title: Bearer JWT authentication
requirements.requirement: When bearer JWT mode is supported, the gateway MUST validate issuer, audience, signature, expiration when applicable, and required gateway/per-tool scopes before listing or invoking protected tools.
negative_checks: Unsigned, expired, wrong-audience, or insufficient-scope JWTs must fail closed.

requirements.title: API key authentication
requirements.requirement: When API key mode is supported, the gateway MUST validate key format/secret/active state/entitlement/quota as claimed and require tenant and actor binding for invocation.
negative_checks: API key mode must not default to anonymous tenant or actor for effect-bearing operations.

requirements.title: Fail-closed tenant and actor binding
requirements.requirement: Every governed invocation MUST bind tenant_id and actor_id from trusted JWT claims, validated API-key headers/env vars, or explicitly configured safe local defaults. Missing, conflicting, or mismatched identity must deny before Runtime execution.
negative_checks: Tenant/actor must not be widened, guessed, synthesized silently, or taken from untrusted payload fields.

requirements.title: Per-tool scope enforcement
requirements.requirement: tools/list and tools/call MUST enforce gateway scopes and per-tool scopes consistently across auth modes and transports.
negative_checks: A client lacking invoke or per-tool scope must not call the tool even if it can list tools unless listing scope explicitly permits it.

requirements.title: Tool descriptor accuracy
requirements.requirement: tools/list MUST return accurate MCP tool descriptors for every advertised tool, including name, description, input schema, and required parameters.
negative_checks: Advertised tools must not appear in tools/list if they cannot be invoked safely or are deferred.

requirements.title: Governed execute tool
requirements.requirement: The gateway MUST implement the advertised governed execute tool surface, including request shape validation and mapping into the Runtime decision/execution contract.
negative_checks: Malformed tool arguments must deny or return validation error before Runtime execution.

requirements.title: Hardening/attestation tool honesty
requirements.requirement: If a hardening or attestation tool is advertised, the repo MUST distinguish real execution from governed stub/demo behavior and tests must match the actual claim.
negative_checks: Stubbed hardening execution must not be claimed as production hardening execution.

requirements.title: Legacy HTTP compatibility remains governed
requirements.requirement: Legacy endpoints such as GET /health, POST /mcp/tools/list, and POST /mcp/tools/invoke MUST remain consistent with gateway governance semantics when advertised.
negative_checks: Legacy invoke must not bypass auth, scope, identity binding, Decide-before-Execute, or receipts.

requirements.title: Canonical legacy schema validation
requirements.requirement: Legacy request and response envelopes MUST be validated against the repo's canonical schema when such schemas are claimed.
negative_checks: Invalid envelope shape must not flow into Runtime execution.

requirements.title: Ingress spine event lifecycle
requirements.requirement: When ingress spine receipts are enabled/claimed, each governed invoke MUST emit or expose the claimed lifecycle events in causal order: directive, intent, request when execute attempted, decision, execution when executed, outcome, and evidence_pack when available.
negative_checks: Outcome must be recorded for deny/failure paths when claimed; execution receipt must not exist when execution did not occur.

requirements.title: Ingress spine persistence modes
requirements.requirement: IngressSpine modes MUST behave as documented: Off disables persistence without pretending durability; BestEffort logs append failures without blocking; Required fails closed when required append operations fail.
negative_checks: Required mode must not call Runtime if directive append fails; BestEffort must not claim durable proof when append failed.

requirements.title: Correlation preservation
requirements.requirement: The gateway MUST preserve or generate a canonical correlation identifier and carry it through ingress event lifecycle, Runtime Decide, Runtime Execute, and structuredContent response.
negative_checks: Correlation IDs must not be regenerated independently per phase such that lineage is broken.

requirements.title: Runtime unavailable fail-closed behavior
requirements.requirement: If Runtime is unavailable, times out, returns invalid content, or throws during Decide or Execute, the gateway MUST fail closed and return a governed error/denial without performing local side-effect execution.
negative_checks: Gateway must not locally execute requested effects as a fallback.

requirements.title: Origin enforcement for browser clients
requirements.requirement: For browser-based HTTP clients, non-loopback origins MUST be blocked by default and only explicitly configured origins may be allowed.
negative_checks: Wildcard browser origins must not be default for governed invocation.

requirements.title: Rate limiting behavior
requirements.requirement: If rate limiting is advertised, it MUST be configurable and tested on public MCP routes without breaking authorized normal traffic.
negative_checks: Rate limiting must not be documented as active if disabled by default and untested.

requirements.title: Health endpoint semantics
requirements.requirement: GET /health MUST accurately report gateway health and downstream Runtime state when claimed.
negative_checks: Health must not report fully healthy when downstream Runtime is unavailable unless status clearly distinguishes degraded runtime.

requirements.title: Secret and local default discipline
requirements.requirement: DefaultBearerToken, DefaultApiKey, JWT public keys, and API keys MUST be documented as local/dev or environment-provided secrets and MUST NOT be committed as real production secrets.
negative_checks: No checked-in real bearer token, private key, API key, or production secret.

requirements.title: Example and demo accuracy
requirements.requirement: Examples and demos MUST be runnable or honestly marked as illustrative/deferred, and trust-failure demos MUST demonstrate runtime-down and deny-without-execute paths when claimed.
evidence_required: demo tests or manual smoke receipt
negative_checks: Broken examples must not be used as proof of production functionality.

requirements.title: Automated test coverage for advertised behavior
requirements.requirement: The repo MUST contain automated tests for core advertised behavior: schema fixtures, approve/execute, deny/no-execute, missing scope, tenant mismatch, runtime unavailable, correlation preservation, spine modes, rate limiting when advertised, MCP initialize, tools/list, tools/call, origin enforcement, and legacy compatibility when advertised.
negative_checks: Test names alone are not evidence; assertions must verify behavior.

requirements.title: CI validates restore, build, test, and smoke coverage
requirements.requirement: CI MUST validate restore/build/test for the gateway and any claimed smoke scripts or Python examples that are part of the release gate.
negative_checks: Deploy must not be treated as validated if PR validation skips core gateway tests.

requirements.title: Deployment safety boundaries
requirements.requirement: If deploy workflows are present, PRs must not deploy to staging, protected main/manual dispatch rules must be respected, and rollback runbooks must exist when claimed.
negative_checks: Fork PRs must not receive deploy credentials or run production/staging deploy.

requirements.title: Deferred feature honesty
requirements.requirement: Features marked deferred, not emitted yet, stubs, or compatibility-only must not be claimed as complete production functionality.
evidence_required: tool descriptors omit deferred features or label them clearly
negative_checks: SSE, resources, prompts, live spine persistence, hardening execution, or remote PR publication must not be claimed complete unless implemented and tested.

requirements.title: Zero-friction adoption claim gating
requirements.requirement: The gateway may claim low-friction MCP adoption only if standard MCP clients can connect using documented HTTP or stdio config without custom client code beyond auth/header/env configuration.
evidence_required: stdio or HTTP client compatibility proof
negative_checks: If only custom legacy HTTP clients work, zero-friction MCP adoption is a false or overstated claim.

required_negative_tests.title: Deny does not execute
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Runtime Execute is never called when Runtime Decide denies.

required_negative_tests.title: Runtime unavailable fails closed
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Runtime outage/timeout/invalid response returns governed error/denial and no local fallback execution.

required_negative_tests.title: Missing scope blocks invoke
required_negative_tests.must_prove: A client without required gateway or per-tool invoke scope cannot call protected tools.

required_negative_tests.title: Tenant mismatch blocks invoke
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Tenant identity mismatch between trusted auth binding and request context is denied before Runtime execution.

required_negative_tests.title: Missing actor or tenant blocks invoke
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Effect-bearing invocation cannot proceed without bound tenant_id and actor_id.

required_negative_tests.title: Invalid JWT blocks protected surfaces
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Unsigned, wrong audience/issuer, expired, or malformed JWT cannot list/invoke protected tools as configured.

required_negative_tests.title: Required spine append failure blocks runtime
required_negative_tests.must_prove: When IngressSpine mode is Required and directive append fails, Runtime is not called.

required_negative_tests.title: BestEffort spine failure is honest
required_negative_tests.must_prove: When BestEffort append fails, response/logs do not falsely claim durable persistence for failed append.

required_negative_tests.title: Legacy invoke cannot bypass governance
required_negative_tests.must_prove: POST /mcp/tools/invoke follows auth, scope, identity, Decide-before-Execute, and receipt behavior.

required_negative_tests.title: Browser origin blocked by default
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Non-loopback browser origins are rejected unless explicitly allowed.

required_negative_tests.title: Malformed tool arguments fail before execution
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Invalid tool arguments/schema never reach Runtime Execute.

required_negative_tests.title: Unsupported MCP version/method safe failure
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Unsupported protocol versions or methods return protocol-safe errors without invoking tools.

required_negative_tests.title: stdio stdout purity
required_negative_tests.must_prove: stdio mode does not write non-protocol logs/banner text to stdout.

required_negative_tests.title: Execution receipt absent when execution absent
required_negative_tests.must_prove: Responses for denial/not-executed paths do not include a misleading execution receipt.

allowed_only_if: tests or smoke receipts prove at least one supported transport
false_if: only legacy custom HTTP endpoints work

allowed_only_if: all invoke surfaces share or enforce equivalent governance path
direct execution bypass search is clean
deny/no-execute and legacy no-bypass tests exist
false_if: tool handlers can call execution directly

allowed_only_if: responses include receipt references when available
spine append behavior exists and is tested for configured modes
failure paths are honest about durability
false_if: spine persistence is deferred but described as complete

allowed_only_if: tenant_id and actor_id come from trusted auth/config channels
missing or conflicting identity blocks invoke
negative tests exist for invalid identity and scopes
false_if: payload-provided tenant/actor is trusted without validation
missing identity defaults silently in non-local/protected mode
scope checks are absent or docs-only

allowed_only_if: documented standard MCP client config works
auth setup is bounded to headers/env vars/token minting
no custom client SDK is required for basic initialize/list/call
false_if: client must use nonstandard protocol extensions for basic calls
examples are stale or not executable

required_fields: jsonrpc or MCP result wrapper as applicable

required_settings_when_claimed: RateLimiting settings if rate limiting is advertised

repo_map_first: Identify language/framework, entrypoint, routes, MCP handlers, auth services, runtime client, tool registry, schemas, tests, examples, and CI.
Use targeted search terms before reading full files.
evidence_rules: Docs are claim evidence only, not implementation evidence.
Test names are not proof unless assertions are inspected.
Mock runtime tests are acceptable for gateway behavior when they prove request ordering and failure handling.
Live Runtime tests are not required for gateway conformance unless the repo claims live integration readiness.
Generated example payloads are not proof unless validated by tests or smoke commands.
Search for bypasses using direct runtime execute calls, direct tool execution services, and legacy endpoints.

remediation_lane_groups.success_condition: Advertised MCP transports and methods are implemented, tested, and protocol-safe.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane B - Auth, identity, and scopes
remediation_lane_groups.success_condition: Invalid/missing identity and scope failures deny before Runtime execution.
Gateway never executes or reports execution without prior approved decision.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane D - Structured envelope and schemas
remediation_lane_groups.success_condition: All outputs preserve canonical governed envelope and honest error semantics.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane E - Ingress spine and receipts
remediation_lane_groups.success_condition: Receipt/spine behavior is ordered, mode-correct, and honest across success, denial, and failure.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane F - Operational hardening and docs truth
forbidden_scope: core governance changes unless required by discovered defect
remediation_lane_groups.success_condition: Operational claims match implementation and tests; unsafe public/deploy defaults are blocked.

final_verdict_policy.conformant: All core requirements implemented with evidence, all required negative tests pass or are explicitly not applicable with justification, and no false claims remain.
final_verdict_policy.conditionally_conformant: No critical failures or false claims; limited medium/low gaps remain with honest deferred labels and no safety-impacting missing tests.
final_verdict_policy.non_conformant: Any critical requirement fails, any protected execution path bypasses governance, any fail-closed invariant fails, or any material public claim is contradicted by repo evidence.
final_verdict_policy.unknown: Repository evidence is insufficient to evaluate core claims; use only when the verifier cannot inspect required files or commands.

notes_for_verify_canon: Keep findings gateway-specific. If a defect belongs to Runtime, Cortex, Collective, or website, mark as integration dependency or out_of_scope.
For gateway verification, the most important proof is call path behavior under failure: denial, missing identity, missing scope, runtime down, and spine append failure.
Treat every advertised transport as a separate verification surface. HTTP MCP passing does not prove stdio, and legacy passing does not prove standard MCP.
Treat deferred README items as acceptable only if not claimed complete elsewhere in the repo.
Do not spawn remediation workers unless explicitly requested.
